raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 697 eliminates the requirement that each physician assistant (PA) work with a single physician.",
    "The bill aims to end disparities between PAs and other medical providers in similar professional regulations.",
    "A recent report from the University of California San Francisco predicts a potential shortfall of 4,700 clinicians in California by 2025.",
    "The California Future Health Workforce Commission's report highlights PAs as a priority profession that can help grow and sustain California's health workforce.",
    "Care organizations employing more PAs and allowing them to provide a full range of primary care services report lower costs and reduced use of services.",
    "Implementing a more flexible work arrangement for PAs could incentivize physicians to utilize their full potential."
  ]
}
```
